hypothesis: If the integrated workflow combining thermodynamic modeling, cellular-automata reaction-network simulation, and commercial availability/pricing data is applied to the Ca–Si–O–C–N–H–Al–B chemical space at 800–1600°C, then it will identify commercially accessible synthesis recipes that produce Ca2SiO4 as the dominant product while minimizing total reagent cost relative to alternative feasible recipes because the combined modules jointly capture reaction energetics, pathway likelihoods, and market constraints.
